channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Future of Trump budget bill uncertain as House GOP rebels mutiny over Senate plan
item.description: The future of congressional Republicans' reconciliation plans is uncertain with an expected vote looming on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP wins reinstatement to White House events after judge rules government can’t bar its journalists
item.description: A federal judge ordered the White House on Tuesday to restore The Associated Press’ full access to cover presidential events, affirming on First Amendment grounds that the government cannot punish the news organization for the content of its speech. U.S. District Judge Trevor N.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department will disband its crypto-related enforcement team
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is disbanding a team of prosecutors who targeted cryptocurrency crimes and is shifting its focus away from complex crypto-related cases involving banking and securities law, according to a memo reviewed by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota pushes back on Trump demand to end DEI or lose school funding
item.description: Minnesota Education Commissioner Willie Jett told federal officials the state already complies with federal civil rights laws and that the Trump administration did not have the power “to unilaterally overrule the will of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Trump fails to sway many House GOP holdouts as his 'big' bill of tax breaks and spending cuts stalls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson found himself in a familiar jam on Tuesday: Conservative Republican holdouts are stalling action on President Donald Trump's "big” bill of tax breaks and spending reductions, refusing to accept a Senate GOP budget framework approved over the weekend becau
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: 'Spine of steel', willing to deal
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration says it cut funding to some life-saving UN food programs by mistake
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department said Tuesday that it had rolled back an undisclosed number of sweeping funding cuts to U.N. World Food Program emergency projects in 14 impoverished countries, saying it had terminated some of the contracts for life-saving aid by mistake.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Hegseth reveal whopping figure they want for the next Pentagon budget
item.description: U.S. military spending would reach a record high under President Donald Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's $1 trillion budget proposal for 2026.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House: US will lead in AI, but China is catching up
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: The speed of China’s innovation in the artificial intelligence space is “accelerating," director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Michael Kratsios told Fox News Digital but maintained that the United States’ “promote and protect" strategy will solidify its sta
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: An immigration judge in Louisiana says she will decide later this week whether the government can continue to detain Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia University activist facing deportation for his role in pro-Palestinian campus protests.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mike Huckabee nomination advances in Senate, one step closer to becoming ambassador to Israel
item.description: The Senate moved closer to confirming Mike Huckabee as ambassador to Israel amid backlash over his past remarks on Palestinians and Trump’s vision for the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump helps plant replacement for a historic White House tree that was removed over safety concerns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday helped shovel dirt onto the replacement for a historic White House tree that had to be removed because of safety concerns around its deteriorating condition. “We have a beautiful tree now at the White House,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Park Service restores original Harriet Tubman, Underground Railroad webpage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The National Park Service has reversed edits and restored content to its webpage about Harriet Tubman and the Underground Railroad in the wake of news reports and public backlash over the changes.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Dem accuses Trump admin of 'fire sale approach' as GOP aims to lower federal gov's real estate portfolio
item.description: Democrats accused the Trump administration of "looting" the federal government during a Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) subcommittee hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's nominee for ambassador to the Vatican defends recent cuts to US foreign aid
item.description: President Donald Trump's nominee for ambassador to the Vatican defended many of the administration's foreign-aid cuts at a Senate hearing Tuesday, even while saying Catholic charitable groups are well-equipped to deliver such aid efficiently.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Trump budget blueprint opponents dig in, China tariff war escalates
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is racing to get House Republicans behind a Senate-passed framework for enacting large swaths of President Trump's domestic agenda, even as conservative opposition to the measure grows.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Conservative website‘s bid to access Sen. Mitchell body camera footage revived by appeals court
item.description: Alpha News sought to compel law enforcement to release certain video related to Sen. Nicole Mitchell’s arrest in April 2024. An appeals court ruling has reopened the possibility of body and dash camera footage being released ahead of her trial.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Growing opposition from House conservatives threatens to derail Trump's agenda
item.description: Growing opposition among hard-line House conservatives to the Senate’s framework for advancing President Trump’s ambitious legislative agenda is threatening to make this week’s vote one of the heaviest lifts yet for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.).
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Duke basketball star could be deported under Trump visa policy
item.description: The State Department announced Saturday that visas of South Sudanese passport holders would be revoked.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to deport Venezuelans under wartime law, but only after judges' review
item.description: In a bitterly divided 5-4 decision, the court said the administration must give Venezuelans who it claims are gang members “reasonable time” to go to court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Congress expresses growing concern on Trump's tariffs
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties on Capitol Hill are voicing growing concern as President Donald Trump's tariffs wreak havoc on Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Senator says he was joking about using violence against journalists
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., suggested that a lot "less false stories" would be written by journalists if they got into physical altercations with politicians.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child
item.description: A second school-aged child in West Texas has died from a measles-related illness, a hospital spokesman confirmed Sunday, as the outbreak continues to swell.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid amid President Donald Trump's planned cuts to the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Last year’s FAFSA cycle turned into chaos as the Biden administration attempted to simplify the forms.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Congress has the power to halt Trump's tariffs. But Republicans aren't ready to use it
item.description: Lawmakers are talking of clawing back their power to levy tariffs, but almost none seemed ready to turn their words into action. The Republican president is upending longstanding GOP principles like support for free trade.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Stock market tumble impacts retirement savings; Florida economists explain
item.description: “This panic, or phase, is not going to last more than 30 days. The stock market, sooner than later, will bounce back,” said Tampa Economist Federico Alves.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump restoring millions in family planning funds for two states
item.description: The Trump administration is restoring millions of dollars in Title X funds to two states after the Biden administration chose to withhold those funds because the pair failed to comply with program rules.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5
